headline: Sarawak Hydropower Exports Bypass Indigenous Communities Without Electricity
context: Sarawak exports 300MW of hydropower to West Kalimantan while 73 longhouse communities within 50km of transmission lines remain without grid electricity.

cards.big: Sarawak exports 300MW of hydropower to Indonesia.
cards.sub: 73 longhouse communities near the transmission lines — some within 5km — have no electricity.

cards.big: SESCO's rural electrification rate in Sarawak is 91%.
cards.sub: The remaining 9% are disproportionately indigenous communities in remote areas — 28,000 people.

cards.big: Export revenue from the Indonesia power sale is RM840M annually.
cards.sub: Connecting 73 longhouses would cost an estimated RM48M — 5.7% of one year's export revenue.

cards.big: MA63 guarantees Sarawak autonomy over electricity supply.
cards.sub: Federal intervention to mandate rural connection is constitutionally barred without state consent.

cards.big: Exporting electricity past communities that have none is a statement about who development is for.
Sarawak's energy sovereignty under MA63 is legitimate. But autonomy comes with responsibility. Connecting 73 longhouses at 5.7% of export revenue is not a resource problem.
